data.body:
Report landed at docs/research/task-decomposition-sota-2026.md (commit 143d5074e). Key results:
- Research verified: CMU-CS-25-132 +24% decomposition gain (12-subtask empirical cap); IBM 2605.15425 static decomposition WORSE than monolithic, runtime-structured -51.7%/-73.3% retry tokens; CodePlan 5/7 vs 0/7; FixedBench 35-65% over-action; CooperBench -30% coordination penalty vs CAID/Shepherd structured gains; Tricorder <10% FP trust cliff; CORE -25.8% FP via verify pass; AQUSA 93.8R/72.2P deterministic ceiling; ambiguity does NOT predict failure but incompleteness/inconsistency do.
- OSS convergence (15 systems, file-level): plan/execute pause, dependency DAG as checkable field, atomicity=reviewability (not size), verification as required structured field, self-contained task packets; contested: parallel-vs-sequential default, rigid grammar vs prose, numeric sizing (almost nobody has one).
- Store history: 1,897 plan reviews/527 tickets, PASS 47%/BLOCK 45%, 30% of candidate findings dropped by pass 2/3, 68% of tickets >=2 rounds, 92% of BLOCKed tickets converge to PASS. Post-claim description edits: 16/505 (3.2%), 15 substantive; of 8 with persisted reviews: 3 MISSED (all asserted-capability-of-existing-module grounding, incl. 1 via bug exemption), 4 CAUGHT-BUT-IGNORED (advisory latency), 1 UNKNOWABLE.
- 7 recommendations (R1-R7) in report section 6, each pass-scoped with falsifiable experiment + FP assessment: asserted-capability probe (P1), operator-attested lint (P1/DET), decomposition-shape container criterion (P1), necessity/no-op gate + bug-tier pilot (P1), agentic capability sub-answer (P2), advisory-triage move (P4), gate-eval instrumentation (cross-cutting; extends docs/experiments/plan-review-gate).
